title: Everyday nouns: choose the right word
lesson: Pick the everyday noun that fits each sentence. These are very common words for people, places, food, and things at home.

items.text: We cook the dinner in the {1}.
items.explanation: The kitchen is the room where we cook food.

items.text: I sleep in my {1} every night.
items.explanation: A bed is the furniture you sleep on.

items.text: She drinks a glass of {1} when she is thirsty.
items.explanation: Water is what you drink when you are thirsty.

items.text: The children go to {1} to learn.
items.explanation: School is the place where children learn.

items.text: Our {1} teaches us English in class.
items.explanation: A teacher is the person who teaches a class.

items.text: He drives his {1} to work.
items.explanation: A car is a road vehicle for driving to work.

items.text: I read a {1} before I go to sleep.
items.explanation: A book is something you read.

items.text: Please open the {1} to let in some air.
items.explanation: A window opens to let air into a room.

items.text: We eat at the {1} in the dining room.
items.explanation: A table is the furniture where you eat meals.

items.text: Our {1} barks when someone comes to the door.
items.explanation: A dog is the pet that barks.

items.text: I eat a red {1} for a healthy snack.
items.explanation: An apple is a common fruit you can eat.

items.text: We put butter on a slice of {1}.
items.explanation: Bread is the food you put butter on.

items.text: The baby drinks warm {1} from a bottle.
items.explanation: Milk is the white drink babies have.

items.text: My {1} is a girl in my family with the same parents as me.
items.explanation: A sister is a female with the same parents.

items.text: When I am sick, I visit the {1}.
items.explanation: A doctor helps you when you are sick.

items.text: We sit on a {1} at the table.
items.explanation: A chair is the furniture you sit on.

items.text: My family lives in a big {1} with a garden.
items.explanation: A house is a building where a family lives.

items.text: I eat my soup with a {1}.
items.explanation: A spoon is the tool you use to eat soup.

items.text: She wears a {1} on each foot.
items.explanation: A shoe goes on your foot.

items.text: What time is it? Look at the {1} on the wall.
items.explanation: A clock tells you the time.

items.text: I write my name with a {1}.
items.explanation: A pen is what you write with.

items.text: We watch films on the {1} in the evening.
items.explanation: A television is the screen you watch films on.

items.text: I keep the cold food in the {1}.
items.explanation: A fridge keeps food cold.

items.text: My {1} cooks dinner for us every day.
items.explanation: Your mother is your female parent.

items.text: The cat drinks from a small {1} of milk.
items.explanation: A bowl is a round dish that holds liquid or food.

items.text: We walk through the {1} to leave the room.
items.explanation: A door is the opening you walk through to leave a room.

items.text: I wash my hands and dry them with a {1}.
items.explanation: A towel dries your wet hands.

items.text: The flowers grow in our {1} behind the house.
items.explanation: A garden is the outdoor space where flowers grow.

items.text: We buy fruit and vegetables at the {1}.
items.explanation: A shop is where you buy things.

items.text: He plays football on the green {1} in the park.
items.explanation: Grass is the green ground in a park.

items.text: At night the {1} and the stars are in the sky.
items.explanation: The moon shines in the sky at night.

items.text: We cut the vegetables with a sharp {1}.
items.explanation: A knife is the tool used to cut food.

items.text: I unlock the front door with my {1}.
items.explanation: A key unlocks a door.

items.text: The fish swim in the {1} near the boat.
items.explanation: Fish swim in the sea.

items.text: My {1} is a girl with the same parents as me.
items.explanation: A sister is a female with the same parents.
